evals.name: basic-uv-migration
evals.prompt: I have a FastAPI project with a requirements.txt from pip freeze. I want to modernize it to use uv with pyproject.toml. The project source is in src/ and tests in tests/. The project name is 'my-fastapi-app'. Please migrate it.
evals.expected_output: plan.md, pyproject.toml with PEP 621 format, reports with AST scan results, requirements.in, summary.md. Direct deps should be: fastapi, httpx, pydantic, redis, python-dotenv, pyyaml, python-multipart, uvicorn. Transitive deps like annotated-types, anyio, h11, etc. should NOT appear in pyproject.toml.

assertions.text: plan.md was generated in the output directory
pyproject.toml uses [project] section with PEP 621 format, not [tool.poetry]
pyproject.toml dependencies include all direct deps: fastapi, httpx, pydantic, redis, python-dotenv, pyyaml, python-multipart, uvicorn
pyproject.toml dependencies do NOT include transitive deps like annotated-types, anyio, h11, httpcore, sniffio, starlette, click, typing-extensions
summary.md was generated in the output directory
requirements.in was generated listing direct dependencies
Original requirements.txt is still present (not deleted)

evals.name: poetry-dockerfile-migration
evals.prompt: I want to migrate this Flask project to use Poetry. The project has a Dockerfile that I also want updated. Source is in src/, tests in tests/. Project name is 'product-api'. Please migrate everything including the Dockerfile.
evals.expected_output: plan.md, pyproject.toml with [tool.poetry] format, poetry-compatible Dockerfile replacing pip install with poetry install, reports with AST scan, summary.md. Direct deps: flask, marshmallow, sqlalchemy, requests, pillow, python-dateutil, gunicorn. Dockerfile should use poetry install --only main.

assertions.text: plan.md was generated in the output directory
pyproject.toml uses [tool.poetry.dependencies] section (Poetry-native format)
pyproject.toml dependencies include all direct deps: flask, marshmallow, sqlalchemy, requests, pillow, python-dateutil, gunicorn
pyproject.toml dependencies do NOT include transitive deps like blinker, certifi, charset-normalizer, itsdangerous, markupsafe, werkzeug, jinja2
Dockerfile uses 'poetry install' instead of 'pip install -r requirements.txt'
Dockerfile copies pyproject.toml (and poetry.lock) for dependency installation, not requirements.txt
pytest is placed in dev dependency group, not in main dependencies
summary.md was generated in the output directory

evals.name: uv-old-deps-dockerfile
evals.prompt: I need to modernize this data science project to use uv. It has a Dockerfile too. Source in src/, tests in tests/. Project name is 'ml-pipeline'. Some of these packages are older and might have build issues — please handle that. Migrate everything.
evals.expected_output: plan.md, pyproject.toml with PEP 621, uv-compatible Dockerfile, reports with AST scan, summary.md. Direct deps: numpy, pandas, scikit-learn, opencv-python, pillow, matplotlib, scipy, requests, pyyaml, python-dateutil, tqdm. Should mention pkg_resources/setuptools build issues and include troubleshooting in plan or summary. Dockerfile should use uv sync.

assertions.text: plan.md was generated in the output directory
pyproject.toml uses [project] section with PEP 621 format
pyproject.toml dependencies include all direct deps: numpy, pandas, scikit-learn, opencv-python, pillow, matplotlib, scipy, requests, pyyaml, python-dateutil, tqdm
pyproject.toml dependencies do NOT include transitive deps like contourpy, cycler, fonttools, kiwisolver, pyparsing, threadpoolctl, six, packaging, certifi
Dockerfile uses 'uv sync' instead of 'pip install -r requirements.txt'
plan.md, summary.md, or pyproject.toml mentions pkg_resources or setuptools build constraint as a potential issue
summary.md was generated in the output directory
